hero.title: QuantumBTC: Bitcoin Post-Quantum Security
hero.subtitle: The Ultimate Lightning Network (LN) Stress-Test
hero.description: Not a game of chance. A provably fair cryptographic BTC experiment funding the future of Bitcoin's post-quantum security.

manifesto.title: The Quantum Manifesto
manifesto.content: Satoshi Nakamoto's vision requires defense against tomorrow's threats. We are using micro-transactions to stress-test the Lightning Network, while funding research into quantum-resistant cryptography.

l1_l2.title: Bitcoin L1 Vault vs Lightning LN Pocket
l1_l2.l1: Bitcoin Layer 1 (L1) is the vault. Slow, expensive, but immutable.
l1_l2.l2: Lightning Network (LN) is your pocket wallet. Instant, extremely cheap, perfect for everyday BTC payments.

wallets.title: Recommended Bitcoin Lightning Wallets (LN)
wallets.muun: Muun Wallet: Best for beginners. Beware of high hidden fees if the Bitcoin network (L1) is congested.
wallets.phoenix: Phoenix Wallet: Excellent non-custodial LN option. Perfect for BTC users in emerging markets.
wallets.wos: Wallet of Satoshi: Custodial but easiest for immediate onboarding. Only store small BTC amounts.

faq.title: Security FAQ
faq.a1: No. This is a stress-taste mechanism designed to extract verifiable entropy and fund open-source research.
faq.a2: We use the Drand network for provable, verifiable randomness. The hash is transparent.

quantum_shield.title: Quantum Sovereignty: The Roadmap to Bitcoin's Future
quantum_shield.intro: While Bitcoin Layer 1 remains the ultimate Decentralized Store of Value, it faces a technical challenge: the emergence of Shor's algorithm on sufficiently large quantum computers. Our mission at QuantumBTC is to research and document the paths for a secure transition.
quantum_shield.title1: 1. The Threat: ECDSA and Public Key Exposure
quantum_shield.desc1: Bitcoin's current digital signature (ECDSA) relies on the hardness of the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem. A quantum computer could derive a private key from a public key in seconds.
quantum_shield.bullet1_1: Most modern BTC addresses (Native SegWit) hide the public key behind a hash, providing baseline protection until the owner spends from it.
quantum_shield.bullet1_2: Vulnerability: Reused addresses, or old P2PK (Pay-to-Public-Key) addresses like Satoshi's original 1M BTC, have their public keys exposed and are the primary targets.
quantum_shield.title2: 2. The Fork Dilemma: Softfork vs. Hardfork
quantum_shield.desc2: We are researching two main paths for protocol protection and value migration:
quantum_shield.softfork_desc: Adding new script types supporting Post-Quantum (PQ) signatures like Dilithium or SPHINCS+. This maintains blockchain continuity but requires users to manually 'shield' their funds by moving them to these new addresses.
quantum_shield.hardfork_desc: A non-backward compatible reboot. This would use a snapshot of the old chain and a 'Burn and Mint' mechanism on the new PQ-protected branch. This is politically complex but technically cleaner.
quantum_shield.title3: 3. The LN Layer: An Experimental Gateway
quantum_shield.strategy: It's important to understand a technical truth: The Lightning Network (LN) is only as secure as the L1. If Bitcoin's underlying cryptography is broken, the 'Justice' mechanism of LN also fails. However, LN acts as our agile laboratory. Its modular nature allows node operators to test 'canary' deployments of NIST-standardized signatures (like Dilithium) within private channels, providing the real-world data needed to safely upgrade the L1 Bóveda.
quantum_shield.title4: How can you protect your BTC today?
quantum_shield.step1: Migrate to Native SegWit (Bech32) to hide your public keys behind a hash.
quantum_shield.step2: Strictly avoid address reuse. No metadata should be leaked to potential attackers.
quantum_shield.step3: Keep your funds in cold storage for long term, but use LN for your daily experimentation.
quantum_shield.step4: Support the stress-test at quantumbtc.dev to directly fund open-source research.
quantum_shield.conclusion: Bitcoin's security is an iterative process. By building the shield today, we ensure Satoshi's legacy lasts for centuries.
